title: 工具账本：Claude 计费和成本变化
original_title: Uber Caps Usage of AI Tools Like Claude Code to Manage Costs
summary: Uber Caps Usage of AI Tools Like Claude Code to Manage Costs I wrote the other day about Uber blowing its 2026 AI budget in four months, and how that wasn't particularly surprising given they would have set that budget in 2025, before anyone could have predicted how popular token-burning coding agents were about to become. Natalie Lung for Bloomberg: The rideshare giant is limiting all employees to $1,500 in monthly token spending per AI coding tool, an Uber spokesperson said in response to a Bloomberg News inquiry. That means spending on one tool doesn’t have a bearing on the budget for another. The limits, which have been instituted in recent months, only apply to agentic coding software such as Cursor or Anthropic PBC’s Claude Code. A $1,500 monthly limit per tool strikes me as a rational policy response to over-spending, and much more sensible than those tokenmaxxing leaderboards enc
source_category_title: AI 工具与开发者平台
topic_direction_description: 公众号、小红书、视频号、Google SEO、AI SEO、平台政策、账号限制、推荐机制和内容分发生态变化。重点看哪些规则会影响流量、账号、合规、内容收益和后续变现。
